result.summary: Mixpanel exposes multiple HTTP APIs for ingestion, querying, export, pipelines, schemas, GDPR, warehouse connectors, and feature flags; the corpus names authentication docs but does not provide enough detail to identify user-minted credentials or binding mechanics. It also references an MCP Server, but no connect endpoint is given.

surfaces.name: Mixpanel Ingestion API
surfaces.notes: Standard server for the Ingestion API. The corpus also lists regional servers `api-eu.mixpanel.com` and `api-in.mixpanel.com`, but only this standard URL is fully specified as a public API surface in the provided pages.

surfaces.name: Mixpanel Query API
surfaces.notes: Standard server for the Query API. The corpus also lists regional servers `eu.mixpanel.com/api` and `in.mixpanel.com/api`.

surfaces.name: Mixpanel Raw Data Export API
surfaces.notes: Standard server for the Raw Data Export API. The corpus also lists regional servers `data-eu.mixpanel.com/api/2.0/export` and `data-in.mixpanel.com/api/2.0/export`.

surfaces.name: Mixpanel Data Pipelines API
surfaces.notes: Standard server for the Data Pipelines API. The corpus also lists regional servers `data-eu.mixpanel.com/api/2.0/nessie` and `data-in.mixpanel.com/api/2.0/nessie`.

surfaces.name: Mixpanel Lexicon Schemas API
surfaces.notes: Standard server for the Lexicon Schemas API. The corpus also states the Warehouse Connectors API uses this same base URL, but it is a separate API family.

surfaces.name: Mixpanel GDPR API
surfaces.notes: Standard server for the GDPR retrieval and deletion API. The corpus also lists regional servers `eu.mixpanel.com/api/app/data-retrievals` and `in.mixpanel.com/api/app/data-retrievals`.

surfaces.name: Mixpanel Feature Flags API
surfaces.notes: Standard server for the Feature Flags API. The corpus also lists regional servers `api-eu.mixpanel.com/flags` and `api-in.mixpanel.com/flags`.
